Instructions for adding a favicon to a Flask application, including HTML and server configuration.

# Purpose
The document provides instructions for adding a favicon to a Flask web application. A favicon is a small icon that browsers use for tabs and bookmarks, helping to identify and brand a website. To implement a favicon, the document suggests placing a 16 × 16 pixel ICO file named `favicon.ico` in the application's static directory. It then explains how to add a link tag in the HTML to ensure browsers can locate the icon. For older browsers that do not support this method, the document describes how to serve the favicon from the website root using a URL rule or a view function with `flask.send_from_directory`. The document also advises configuring the web server to serve the favicon directly if possible.
